CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-04-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees After Special Motion To Strike Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS' JOHN FLORES, PATRICE FLORES, BRITELITE IMMERSIVE INC.'s Motion For Attorneys Fees After Special Motion To Strike Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation.

Cross-Defendants' motion for attorneys' fees is granted in the amount of $46,504.00 in attorneys' fees and $371.00 in costs. Cross-Defendants are entitled to a mandatory award of attorneys' fees in connection with their successful anti-SLAPP motion. (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(c).) Cross-Complainants Dennis Wanken and Eleni Wanken baselessly and unprofessionally accuse moving parties of "an interest in inflating the amount claimed" and of "attempts to pad the bill," and assert that because the case was "not complex," an award of $25,000 would be more appropriate. The Court, having reviewed counsel's briefing and argument in connection with the special motion to strike, disagrees. The Court finds that counsel's hourly rates are reasonable and that the overall amount of time spent by counsel is reasonable in light of the factual and legal issues presented by the motion and the cross-complaint.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Unverified Cross-Complaint As Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan J. Meckfessel, Esq. of Skootsky & Der LLP, T: 415.979.9800, for the cross defendants /moving party;
David I. Kelvin, Esq., T: 510.393.9170, for cross complainants/opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANTS JOHN FLORES, PATRICE FLORES, BETH EHRLICH, KENNETH SMITH'S Special Motion To Strike Unverified Cross-Complaint As Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation, as follows:

Cross-defendants John Flores, Patrice Flores, Beth Ehrlich, and Kenneth Smith's special motion to strike cross-complainants Dennis Wanken and Eleni Wanken's cross-complaint pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute, Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16, et seq., is granted.

[Refer to word minutes for complete ruling.]

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Off Calendar Oct-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan J. Meckfessel, Esq of Skootsky & Der LLP, Telephone (415) 979-9821, appearing for Plaintiff John Flores;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN FLORES, PATRICE FLORES, BRITELITE IMMERSIVE INC.'s Motion For Leave To Issue And Serve Subpoenas Duces Tecum Before Service Of Summons.

Motion granted. Plaintiffs have shown good cause under CCP 2025.210 for issuance of a subpoena to Google before service of the complaint on any defendants.

Order is signed.
